content.description:
Hobson has three layers: the **kernel**, the **viewport**, and **userland**. Each layer builds on the one below it. Understanding this split is the key to knowing where things live and where to look when you want to change something.

## The Three Layers

| Layer | Role | Can you modify it? |
|-------|------|--------------------|
| **Kernel** | Data, events, code loading | Possible but risky ‚Äî use [safe mode](item://33333333-7777-0000-0000-000000000000) to recover |
| **Viewport** | Rendering, navigation, all UI | Yes ‚Äî these are regular items, hot-reloadable |
| **Userland** | Views, libraries, types, your data | Yes ‚Äî this is where you work |

## Kernel

The kernel is the smallest possible runtime that makes everything else work. It has no UI (except a [safe mode](item://33333333-7777-0000-0000-000000000000) fallback screen). It provides four things:

| Responsibility | Module | What it does |
|----------------|--------|--------------|
| **Storage** | [kernel:storage](item://33333333-2222-0000-0000-000000000000) | Persists items in IndexedDB, validates type chains, handles queries |
| **Events** | [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) | Type-hierarchy-aware event bus ‚Äî `item:updated`, `item:created`, `item:deleted`, and custom events |
| **Code loading** | [kernel:module-system](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000) | `api.require('name')` loads code items as ES modules with caching and circular dependency detection |
| **Bootstrap** | [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) | Initializes storage, applies [styles](item://33333333-8888-0000-0000-000000000000), emits `kernel:boot-complete` so the viewport can take over |

The kernel also defines the base [API object](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=createAPI) that all other code uses ‚Äî item CRUD, events, `require()`, parent-child operations. This is the API you see in the [REPL](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000).

The kernel's design principle: **if it can be built as a userland item, it should be.** See [Kernel](item://c0c0c0c0-0001-0000-0000-000000000000) for the full philosophy.

## Viewport

The viewport is the application shell ‚Äî everything you see on screen. It is built entirely from regular items (not kernel code), but most users will never need to modify it.

The viewport handles two broad concerns:

### Rendering

The [viewport-rendering](item://f1111111-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) library is the rendering engine. It turns items into DOM:

- **View resolution** ‚Äî decides which view to use for an item (preference hierarchy, type chain lookup, fallback). See [Views & Rendering](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000004).
- **Render instance registry** ‚Äî tracks every rendered DOM node so the system can do partial re-renders when items change
- **Enriched API** ‚Äî adds rendering methods (`renderItem`, `rerenderItem`, `navigate`, `getViews`, etc.) on top of the kernel's base API. Views receive this enriched API; the REPL gets only the kernel primitives.
- **Reactive re-rendering** ‚Äî listens for `item:updated` events and re-renders affected views, filtered by [selectors](item://c0c0c0c0-0062-0000-0000-000000000000) so only relevant changes trigger work

### Chrome

The remaining viewport items provide the application frame:

| Item | What it does |
|------|--------------|
| [viewport-manager](item://f1111111-0002-0000-0000-000000000000) | Navigation (URL sync, history, root item management) |
| [viewport-view](item://bd74da77-a459-454a-b001-48685d4b536d) | The top-level view ‚Äî renders chrome, decorates items, manages selection highlighting |
| [selection-manager](item://f1111111-0001-0000-0000-000000000000) | Tracks which item is selected and its parent |
| [keyboard-shortcuts](item://f1111111-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) | Global shortcuts (Ctrl+K, Ctrl+?) |
| [item-palette](item://f1111111-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) | The Ctrl+K search/navigation dialog |
| [repl-ui](item://f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) | The REPL panel (Ctrl+\\) |
| [help-dialog](item://f1111111-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) | The Ctrl+? shortcut reference |

See [Viewport](item://c0c0c0c0-0033-0000-0000-000000000000) for full details.

## Userland

Everything else. This is where the system's real value lives:

- **[Views](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000)** ‚Äî how items get displayed (note view, spatial canvas, list view, etc.)
- **[Libraries](item://66666666-0000-0000-0000-000000000000)** ‚Äî reusable code loaded via `api.require()`
- **[Type definitions](item://11111111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000)** ‚Äî what kinds of items exist (note, tag, container, etc.)
- **Your items** ‚Äî notes, tasks, workspaces, anything you create

Userland items are fully inspectable and modifiable. You can change how notes render, create new types, add libraries ‚Äî the system is designed for this. See [Core Concepts](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000002) for how items, types, views, and libraries fit together.

## How They Connect

At boot, the flow is:

1. The [bootloader](item://c0c0c0c0-0002-0000-0000-000000000000) (the HTML file) loads `kernel:core` from IndexedDB
2. The kernel initializes storage, loads styles, renders the safe mode screen as a fallback
3. The kernel emits `kernel:boot-complete`
4. `viewport-manager` (listening for that event) takes over ‚Äî loads the rendering engine, resolves the root item from the URL, and renders the full UI
5. From here, everything is reactive ‚Äî item changes trigger targeted re-renders via the event system

At runtime, the dependency flows downward:

```
  Userland (views, libraries, your items)
      |
      | uses enriched API (renderItem, navigate, ...)
      v
  Viewport (rendering engine, navigation, chrome)
      |
      | uses base API (get, set, query, require, events)
      v
  Kernel (storage, events, module system)
```

## Where to Go From Here

- New to Hobson? Start with [Getting Started](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000005)
- Want to understand items, types, views? See [Core Concepts](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000002)
- Want to build views? See [Views & Rendering](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000004)
- Want to understand events and reactivity? See [Events System](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000006)
- Want the full architecture? See [Architecture Overview](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000003)
- Want the philosophy? See [Philosophy & Inspirations](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000001)
